data.title: Most and least shorted large-cap stocks
data.description:
Short interest across mid- to mega-cap stocks highlights a sharp divide in investor sentiment, with some names drawing heavy bearish bets while others face almost no short pressure at all.

The list outlined below shows short interest ranging from nearly 35% at the high end to below 1% at the low end, underscoring where traders see elevated downside risk—or, conversely, relative stability and confidence—across the market.

Top 10 most shorted mid- to mega-cap stocks (as a % of shares outstanding):

  • Under Armour (UAA) – 34.96% 
  • Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) – 32.23%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) – 31.55%
  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) – 30.88%
  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – 30.60%
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN) – 28.59%
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) – 27.45%
  • Upstart Holdings (UPST) – 27.42%
  • Applied Digital (APLD) – 27.27%
  • Kohl’s (KSS) – 25.86%

Here’s a list of 10 least shorted mid-to-mega cap stocks (short interest 0.5% and above):

  • Ardagh Metal Packaging (AMBP) – 0.50%
  • Greif (GEF.B) – 0.53%
  • The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son (NTB) – 0.53%
  • DRDGOLD (DRD) – 0.57%
  • Brookfield Wealth Solutions (BNT) – 0.61%
  • U-Haul Holding (UHAL.B) – 0.66%
  • Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile (SQM) – 0.66%
  • CNA Financial (CNA) – 0.68%
  • Amazon.com (AMZN) – 0.69%
  • Walmart (WMT) – 0.69%

data.title: 3M Company Q4 earnings preview: Sales growth and margin expansion in focus
data.description:
3M (MMM) is set to announce fourth quarter earnings on Tuesday, and investors will watch out for the company’s sales growth, margin expansions, and 2026 guidance amid a muted macro environment.

Wall Street expects the Post-it maker to post an EPS of $1.80, a 7.1% year-over-year increase, while revenue is expected to rise 3.6% to $6.01B during the quarter.

3M’s CEO & Chairman William Brown, during the Q3 earnings call, raised FY EPS guidance to a range of $7.95 to $8.05 from $7.75 to $8.00. He also updated margin expansion expectations to 180-200 basis points for the year and sales growth of over 2%.

Seeking Alpha analyst William Davis rated the stock a Buy, saying that 3M is executing a turnaround, leveraging restructuring and operational improvements to drive margin expansion and organic growth.

“Despite sluggish top-line growth and legal headwinds, MMM’s superior margins, robust cash flow, and stable financial base support a bullish outlook,” Davis added.

In the past year, 3M shares gained over 24%, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which gained nearly 17% during the same period.

Seeking Alpha's Quant and analysts recommended the stock as a Hold, while the Wall Street analysts rated the company as a Buy.

However, JP Morgan downgraded 3M to Neutral from Overweight, saying the brokerage firm is cautious heading into the quarter as it believes the exit rate on revenue growth could be a disappointment coming away.

“3M’s hands are relatively tied in terms of capital allocation to drive above-average EPS growth given PFAS, and if they start doing dilutive divestitures (recall another 10% of the portfolio is considered 'non-core' with 2-3% being divested in pieces), that would be further downside to EPS growth,” JP Morgan analyst C. Stephen Tusa said, setting a price target of $182.   

Over the last two years, MMM has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen two upward revisions against 10 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen six upward revisions versus four downward moves.

data.title: Netflix's Ted Sarandos reassures Warner deal won't impact theatrical industry
data.description:
Ted Sarandos, the co-chief executive of streaming giant Netflix Inc. (NFLX), has reassured that the theatrical industry will not be impacted by the company's $82.7B deal to acquire the streaming and studio assets of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. (WBD).

In an extensive interview published in The New York Times on Friday, Sarandos said Hollywood critics have misunderstood the company's intentions ever since the deal was announced but also acknowledged that the concerns they raised about the deal were "rightfully so." 

"I understand that folks are emotional about it because they love it and they don’t want it to go away. And they think that we’ve been doing things to make it go away. We haven’t," Sarandos commented when asked about the skepticism people had about the new commitment to the theatrical business.

Speaking further on the topic, Sarandos said the general economics of the theatrical business were more positive than he had seen and modeled.

"We weren’t in that business (theatrical) not because we hated it. We weren’t in that business because our business was doing so well," he said. "I did not get in this business to hurt the theatrical business. I got into this business to help consumers, to help movie fans. I’ve got a great relationship with theater owners."

Sarandos told NYT that Netflix has historically been a builder and not a buyer of companies, but when Warner Bros. put itself up for sale, turning a blind eye to that would have been "reckless."

"When this deal closes, we will own a theatrical distribution engine that is phenomenal and produces billions of dollars of theatrical revenue that we don’t want to put at risk. We will run that business largely like it is today, with 45-day windows. I’m giving you a hard number," Sarandos said. "If we’re going to be in the theatrical business, and we are, we’re competitive people—we want to win. I want to win opening weekend. I want to win box office."

Sarandos said Netflix has the resources the David Zaslav-run media company has been lacking, and once the deal closes, they will keep Warner Bros. running, releasing movies in theaters the way they always have, and keep HBO completely intact.

data.title: Pacifica Silver upsizes brokered offering to about $20.0M
data.description:
• Pacifica Silver (CSE: PSIL) (OTCQB: PAGFF) on Friday said that it has increased the size of its previously announced brokered private placement to raise gross proceeds of up to $20.01 million, citing strong investor demand.
  • The upsized offering will consist of 13.8 million common shares priced at $1.45 each.
  • The company has also granted agents an option to sell up to an additional 15% of the offering for up to $3.0 million in additional proceeds.
  • Proceeds will be used to advance exploration and drilling at its Claudia project in Durango, Mexico, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 23, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • PAGFF is +2.58% to $1.19.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: State Street Q4 earnings beat on NII, inflows; issues 2026 guidance
data.description:
State Street (STT) shares dipped 3.1% in Friday morning trading after posting above-consensus guidance for expenses, although Q4 2025 earnings and revenue each came in better than expected.

The custodial bank sees 2026 expenses rising 3%-4% Y/Y, compared with the +1.6% Visible Alpha estimate. Also for the full year, it sees net interest income growing in the low-single-digits from a year before, vs. +3.8% Visible Alpha consensus, and fee revenue rising 4%-6%.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $2.97, exceeding the $2.84 average analyst estimate, rose from $2.78 in Q3 and $2.60 a year ago.

Q4 revenue of $3.67B, also beating the $3.62B expected, advanced from $3.55B in the prior quarter and $3.41B in Q4 2024.

Net interest income increased 12% sequentially and 7% Y/Y to $802M, surpassing the $765M Visible Alpha consensus.

In Q4, said Chairman and CEO Ron O'Hanley, "we achieved record fee and total revenue, reflecting consistent year-over-year growth, with continued strong performance across Investment Services, Investment Management, and our Markets franchise."

Total net inflows of $85B climbed from $26B in Q3 and $64B in Q4 2024.

Investment Servicing assets under custody or administration rose 4% Q/Q and 16% Y/Y to $53.8T. The annual gain was mainly driven by market appreciation and flows, State Street said.

Total expenses were $2.74B, up from $2.43B in Q3 and $2.44B in the year-earlier period.

Provision for credit losses slipped to $8M from $9M in Q3 and $12M in 2024's Q4.

Conference call at 11:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Sinclair says Scripps refuses to engage on takeover attempt
data.description:
Sinclair Inc. (SBGI) said E.W. Scripps (SSP)  continues to rebuff its takeover attempts.

“Over the last few weeks, Sinclair has continued to reinforce to Scripps its willingness to engage on a proposed Sinclair-Scripps combination," Sinclair said in a statement  on Friday. "Scripps has refused the invitations to speak with its single largest shareholder and instead has stated its preference to execute its standalone plan."

The latest update comes after Scripps (SSP) last month rejected Sinclair's $7 a share cash and stock offer.  Semafor reported last month that Sinclair (SBGI) may start a fight for board seats this year after its offer was rejected. Sinclair first disclosed in November that it had taken a more than 8% stake in Scripps.

Sinclair (SBGI) also said on Friday that the previously announced strategic review of its broadcast business and work related to the separation of Ventures will continue.

Shares of Scripps fell 1.5%, while Sinclair edged higher by 0.4%.

data.title: Paychex announces new up to $1B stock buyback program
data.description:
• Paychex (PAYX) on Friday announced an up to $1B share buyback program.
  • The company said the new plan replaces its 2024 authorization to repurchase $400M of common stock.
  • In fiscal 2025, Paychex returned over $1.5B to shareholders through cash dividends and share repurchases.
  • Shares were +0.04% pre-market to $110.56.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canaan receives Nasdaq notice for minimum bid price deficiency
data.description:
• Canaan (CAN) received a Nasdaq notice on January 14, 2026, for non-compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement after its ADSs traded below that level for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The company has been granted a 180-day compliance period, until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • Canaan stated it will monitor its share price and take reasonable measures, including a potential reverse stock split if necessary, to regain compliance.
